summary_en: Year-end dividend raised ¥3/share to ¥68 (full-year ¥128). DOE 4.0%. Mid-term plan targets DOE 4%+ and stable continuous dividend growth; record consolidated earnings triggered the hike.
title_en: Notification of Revision to Dividend Forecast for Fiscal Year Ending March 2026 (Dividend Increase)
translation_en_brief: Restar Corporation announced on 14 May 2026 a revision to its dividend forecast for the fiscal year ending 31 March 2026. The year-end dividend per share has been increased from the previously forecast ¥125 to ¥128, representing a ¥3 hike. Combined with the interim dividend of ¥68 (unchanged), the full-year dividend reaches ¥128 per share, yielding a dividend-on-equity (DOE) of 4.0%. The increase reflects the achievement of record consolidated results announced the same day. The company stated that its medium-term management plan through March 2027 positions DOE of 4% or higher and stable, continuous dividend growth as core shareholder-return principles, balancing capital returns with investment in growth and financial soundness. The board is scheduled to formally resolve the dividend at its meeting on 29 May 2026.
